exercises.heading: Part 1:Photography
exercises.description: IS PHOTOGRAPHY AN ART FORM? {v} Photography is all around these days and now that everyone has digital cameras and smartphones, everyone is able to take a good photo without much skill. Nonetheless, photography using film or even with a SLR (single lens reflex) camera does require talent and the question on whether it is an art form has been discussed for a long time. Nowadays people would regard photography as being a form of art, however it wasn't always the case and this art form had to fight for its place to be considered a discipline such as drawing, painting, theatre and dance. {v} ORIGIN OF PHOTOGRAPHY IN THE ARTS? {v} Since photography came about it created a storm in the world of art, much like that of the printing press. Many people argue that photography has changed the way we see the world for example photojournalism has been very influential in capturing events. If we think of the dramatic images captured on the front of a newspaper of world events such as war, terrorist attacks, protests etc, a lot of times these said images are more striking than the words used to describe it. {v} The argument around whether photography should be considered an art form centred on the notion that a mechanical medium had no place amongst traditional expressive forms of art. {v} THE DEBATE OF THE ROLE OF PHOTOGRAPHY IN ART. {v} The discussion regarding this came mainly from England and France, the three main arguments were that since it is a chemical and mechanical process it couldn't be considered art. The second, was that it wasn’t equal in creativity. Lastly it was argued that photography was similar to etching or lithography therefore had a place in the world of art and as well as this could be a beneficial medium for arts and as well as positive influence on general culture. {v} STEPS TOWARDS BEING CONSIDERED FINE ‘ART’. {v} During the 1850s the first photographic societies were created which still exist now such as the ‘’Royal Photographic Society’’. This helped to promote the art form and ensured that exhibition spaces included photography as well as paintings. Nonetheless selection committees were reluctant to host photography prints in exhibitions alongside paintings and they still received much criticism from the press. {v} In order to be taken seriously as an art form, photographers attempted to distort the reality of a photographic image. One way of doing this is called composition. This is where an image is made using two or more photos and layering them, taking out the unwanted detail to then reveal a final photo. This need to be seen as a ‘’high art’’ also brought about the concept of still life photography which is the capturing of a group of small inanimate objects such as food photography. {v} Furthermore, to overcome the sharp definition which was seen as too close to reality to be viewed as art. Photographers would smudge the lens, kick the tripod during exposure or use inferior optical elements. As a result, the image would distort the reality and require more of the use of imagination on the viewers part to understand the image. {v} PHOTOGRAPHY TODAY. {v} Now it is considered its own art discipline, this can be supported by the number of well respected museums which host photographic prints not alongside paintings but as an exhibition or section in itself. Museums such as the Tate Modern in London, and the FOAM in Amsterdam are popular sites to get a glimpse of the most talked about photographic pieces of the moment. {v} In addition,In 2011, German artist Andreas Gursky’s Rhein II was auctioned at Christie's in New york for $4,338,500 which broke the records at the time as the most expensive photograph ever sold. It shows a river scene without people and buildings and has been described as a ‘romantic hymn to the grandeur of the Rhine River’. {v} In conclusion, photography with its myriad of uses and in photojournalism, as art, or a marketable commodity is everywhere and has had an undeniable role to play in society and culture. Not only has it become a fine art discipline which can be taught and studied in prestigious roles and schools but it has also helped define our society and culture, an effect of which should not be understated.

exercises.heading: Part 2: Bollywood
exercises.description: WHY IS BOLLYWOOD SUCH A POWERFUL INDUSTRY? {v} PARAGRAPH A. {v} One of India's most financially lucrative industries as well as one of the largest producers of films each year goes by the name of Bollywood. Most people connect this name to a film which portrays a very colourful and romantic storyline featuring scenes of dancing and drama and almost always coming to a happy ending. As its name might suggest, Bollywood is somewhat of a play on Hollywood, the ever so popular cinematic industry in the United States. It took time for Bollywood to get off its feet as it slowly began taking a feather out of the American film industry to revolutionary ideas for its own. In 1931 the first talkie film was released which marked a key point in the production. It was in the mid 1900s when Bollywood began to shape into what it has grown to be today. Films started focusing on lower class situations in India and were strongly influenced by social issues that were going on in the world at that time. Nowadays, most Bollywood films follow the guidelines of the Masala film, which is a style that uses a hodgepodge of genres such as action, drama and comedy. {v} PARAGRAPH B. {v} Many people struggle to understand why it is that Bollywood has gained the level of popularity that it holds today. First of all, Bollywood is profoundly known for the dance numbers that can be seen in practically every single film. The complexity of the choreography as well as the amount of participants is absolutely outstanding and impossible not to admire. This feature is a staple in Bollywood films. Secondly, Bollywood allows viewers to appreciate the change and growth of theatre over time. Many of the actors and actresses who originally starred in Bollywood movies came from Parsi theater tradition in which everything was exaggerated. This concept has still been maintained in current Bollywood features, but not to the extent that makes viewers feel like the movies are too old-style. A third reason is that Bollywood movies realistically address current issues that mainstream cinema may avoid. Bollywood has educated many viewers about what goes on behind the scenes of the picture-perfect life people like to imagine. It is especially educational when it comes to the caste system in India and any social or political struggles the citizens have had to face. {v} PARAGRAPH C. {v} Not only has Bollywood had an impact on Western cultures but it has also had an impact on the Indian culture itself. Fashion has been largely impacted by Bollywood movies as people tend to want to wear what they see on the screen, causing the fashion industry to creatively produce versions of outfits that are seen on TV. Because of these, certain clothing or statements have become relevant in Indian culture. Society itself has been influenced by these movies as it allows directors to bring to light things that may be a challenge in society, therefore forcing people to think about them and take action. Films can have quite a powerful impact on a society and one might say that Bollywood films have allowed the Indian culture to face societal issues throughout time. {v} PARAGRAPH D.{v} Due to the fact that Bollywood is seen as the Indian Hollywood, it’s logical that certain stereotypes about the members of the West and India are formed through this film industry. There are numerous Bollywood films which address this concept by portraying the story of an Indian living in a Western country while maintaining their own Indian culture. On top of that, there are films that look at this role in reverse - a Westerner in Indian culture. It seems that when looking at the Western culture from an Indian perspective, there is little difference between individual cultures. That is to say that the Statue of Liberty might as well be the Big Ben or the Eiffel Tower as long as they are all representations of Western culture. The Indian culture, as seen by Westerns, is filled with strict tradition and different levels of society. These things can be seen in many Bollywood films. {v} PARAGRAPH E.{v} The appeal of a Bollywood film may be controversial to some, but its international success is undeniable when looking at the numbers. A leading reason as to why Bollywood is so popular in the West is because it allows people to fuel their curiosity about life and cultures beyond their own. While other countries do hold their own film industries which portray their culture, none has been able to reach so many crevices of the world as successfully as Bollywood. {v} PARAGRAPH F.{v} Critical thinkers question whether or not Bollywood should take their influence and power to focus less on social issues and more on environmental issues as India itself is in danger of being negatively impacted by things such as climate change. While it may not be the center of focus when observing the environmental decay, it is a fact that film industries do leave a carbon footprint and therefore, should be making people aware of this while taking action to minimize it. More specifically, the heart of Bollywood, Mumbai, is in a risky position as, with climate change, it could be at high risk from sea-level rise. One might argue that in order to save the Bollywood industry, Bollywood must take part in calling for help in saving itself by building awareness and promoting action amongst its fans.
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

exercises.heading: Part 3: Lifelong Learning
exercises.description: KNOWLEDGE FOR EITHER PERSONAL OR PROFESSIONAL REASONS {v} PARAGRAPH A.{v} Formal education tends to be a government requirement in most parts of the world. Traditional education has been and still is one of the leading forms of education that can be found in schools. Most people will complete their schooling up to a certain level and then go out into the world to work a job. The type of learning found in a school and, later on, in a workplace is quite standard textbook learning and may not push the human mind to mature in different ways. Throughout life, humans develop curiosities about life and the world - questions that cannot be answered in a classroom. The work to discover these answers and take our learning outside the box in sometimes unconventional ways is coined by the term lifelong learning. A lifelong learner is someone who, even outside of their school or workplace, strives to learn more about the world and anything else that might be of interest to them. {v} PARAGRAPH B.{v} Why does anyone need lifelong learning in the first place? If an individual is able to function well without searching outside of their career field, why would lifelong learning be beneficial to them? First of all, in pushing yourself to learn about different fields, you can increase your own value in the workplace, ultimately opening doors for yourself. Secondly, many people struggle with finding satisfaction in their life while working a regular 9-5 job and having a set routine. Being a lifelong learner may reveal to you something that brings more vindication, ultimately bringing joy and meaning to life. Thirdly, lifelong learning is actually proven to enhance one's longevity. Keeping the mind active can be compared to keeping the body active - it is good for our health. Lastly, with the speed at which the world changes, it is important to always be striving to learn in order to keep up with the times. {v} PARAGRAPH C.{v} It’s easy to understand how being a lifelong learner can have a positive impact on our lives, but taking the first step to becoming a lifelong learner is where it gets onerous. People wonder how exactly they can become a lifelong learner if they don’t have time to go out and explore in a hands on way. However, it is important to realize that lifelong learning does not have to be time consuming or extreme. {v} PARAGRAPH D.{v} One of the most essential ways to convert yourself into a lifelong learning is by becoming a reader. Reading is one of the best ways one can learn and allows people to expand their mind without leaving the comfort of their own home. Even spending as much as 30 minutes a day reading a book that gives you insight into a new field can be beneficial. Another point to consider is who you are surrounding yourself with. Keeping company with people who push you to analyze and grow is another way to become a lifelong learner and spark your interest to learn even more! Sometimes it is difficult to keep track of the extra things that you might be interested in, therefore making a list of things that pull at your mind sometimes is a good way to keep things organized. When you have spare time you will then be able to focus directly on something that you know will not only profit your mind, but interest you. {v} PARAGRAPH E.{v} Being a lifelong learner on the whole has its benefits, but there are certain skills that, when developed, are especially applicable. The first is creativity, practicing creative tasks and developing your creativity is something that you can use in many different situations. Creativity comes in all shapes and sizes, so finding your visionary niche is the key. The world is full of problem-solvers and, many times, they are the type of people who push for advancement in society as they look for the solution to all types of dilemmas. Developing this type of skill through lifelong learning will be beneficial not only to you in your life, but to the people who you work and interact with. The importance of communication cannot be stressed enough so looking to learn how to enhance your communication skills cannot harm you. {v} PARAGRAPH F.{v} The concept of lifelong learning is known to have incredible benefits and important figures like Albert Einstein, Henry Ford and Mahatma Gandhi were advocates of lifelong learning. The primary benefit is that it increases your brain health, delaying any health issues that might normally come with age. The brain is ever changing and each time you lose information, you must fill that space with something new. Lifelong learning can not only be salubrious physically, but socially. When looking to learn new things, you will run into people who either have or are searching for that same knowledge as you. This will open doors to connect to new people and influence or be influenced by a larger circle of intellectuals.

title: Bright Lights
description: Explore photography, film, and global culture — argue your perspective, decode visuals, and speak with confidence.
